title: Permission Policy
description: A permission policy in the auth system. Policies are data, not code — adding a new permission is adding a row, not writing a conditional. The contract is (subject, resource, action) -> effect where resource and action are extensible types, not hardcoded enums. If a new category of permission emerges, it should be expressible within this model by defining new resource types and action types — not by changing the model itself. Source: auth-spec.md.

id.description: Unique identifier for this policy rule. Generated by the system. Used for management operations (grant, revoke, list).

subject.description: The identity this policy applies to. References an identity ID from identity.json. Can also be a wildcard ('*') for policies that apply to all actors of a given type, or a type-prefixed wildcard ('type:collaborator') for role-based policies.
subject.$comment: Cross-references identity.json via the id field

resource.description: What this policy controls access to. Extensible — not a hardcoded enum. Resource types include: memory paths (e.g., 'memory:/life/finances/*'), tool names (e.g., 'tool:read_file'), system actions (e.g., 'system:create_page'), external services (e.g., 'external:calendar'). New resource types can be defined without changing this schema.

action.description: What operation this policy permits or denies. Extensible — not a hardcoded enum. Common actions: read, write, delete, execute, delegate, approve, configure. New action types can be defined without changing this schema.

effect.description: Whether this policy allows or denies the action. Deny takes precedence over allow when both match (fail-closed). The system starts maximally constrained — the owner loosens it as they gain confidence.

conditions.description: Optional conditions that must be met for this policy to apply. Extensible for future refinement — rate limits, time windows, approval requirements. E.g., { 'max_per_day': 100 } or { 'requires_approval': true }.

created.description: When this policy was created (ISO 8601). Included for audit trail.

created_by.description: Identity ID of who created this policy. References identity.json. Owner or delegator.
created_by.$comment: Cross-references identity.json via the id field
